concerns.text: The plan cites 'BigCode (arxiv)' (URL: .../starcoder2data-extras/.../arxiv/train-00000-of-00044.parquet) as a source for 'Contextual examples for few-shot'. This dataset contains raw training corpora (code/text pairs) and does not provide the explicit Python-to-JavaScript translation pairs required for the few-shot examples. Using raw training data as few-shot examples for a translation task is inappropriate because the model cannot infer the target language (JS) from a source-only or mixed corpus without explicit translation pairs. The plan must identify a dataset containing verified Python->JS translation pairs for the few-shot condition.

The plan lists 'JavaScript (test)' (URL: .../hchautran/javascript/...) as a 'Reference JS code' for 'quality comparison if needed'. This dataset contains standalone JavaScript code, not Python-to-JavaScript translation pairs. It cannot serve as a reference for evaluating the correctness of a translation task (comparing generated JS to expected JS derived from a specific Python source). The plan needs a dataset that provides parallel Python/JS pairs to serve as the ground truth for the 'expected_js_reference' variable.
